mandates.mandate: YOLO never bypasses self-critique. Even in YOLO mode, a below-threshold score triggers retry and potential escalation.
Max 1 retry. No infinite loops. Draft → critique → if low, re-draft once → critique again → if still low, escalate.
The confidence score is the agent's honest assessment. Do not inflate scores to avoid escalation.
The threshold is read from global.yaml disciplines.self_critique_threshold (default 0.85).

step.title: Read the draft output
step.action: Re-read the complete draft output that is about to be persisted (the template-output content).

step.title: Evaluate confidence
step.action: Evaluate the draft against: (a) the step's stated goal from instructions.xml, (b) the workflow's acceptance criteria from checklist.md, (c) the agent persona's domain expertise standards.
Assign a confidence score from 0.0 to 1.0 reflecting how well the output meets all three criteria.
Be honest. Common reasons for low confidence: missing required sections, unsupported claims, contradictions with upstream artifacts, incomplete coverage of acceptance criteria, weak reasoning.

step.title: Threshold check
step.action: Read the threshold from global.yaml: disciplines.self_critique_threshold (default 0.85 if absent).
step.branch: Return PASS with the score. The output may be persisted.

step.title: Retry once
step.action: Identify the specific weak areas that drove confidence below threshold.
Re-draft the output with explicit attention to those weak areas. Do not start from scratch — revise the weak sections.
Re-evaluate confidence on the new draft (repeat step 2 logic).
step.branch: Return PASS with the new score. The revised output replaces the original draft.

step.title: Escalate to user
step.action: Present to the user: (a) the output draft, (b) the confidence score, (c) a summary of what feels wrong and why confidence remains low.
Offer three options: [a]pprove (override — accept the output as-is), [r]eject (abort the step), [e]dit (user edits the output inline).
Wait for user response. Do not auto-proceed.
On approve: return PASS (user-overridden). On reject: return FAIL — the workflow engine will HALT. On edit: persist the user's edited version and return PASS.
